title: Thelypteris straminea
taxonomy: Thelypteris straminea belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae and is classified as a species of fern. More specifically, it is a member of the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of epipetric ferns native to tropical regions. The classification of Thelypteris straminea is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its frond shape and venation pattern. Thelypteris straminea's classification also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within the family Thelypteridaceae. Through phylogenetic analysis, researchers have been able to reconstruct the evolutionary history of Thelypteris straminea and its close relatives.
uses: Thelypteris straminea, also known as the straw spleenwort, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Thelypteris straminea, also known as the straw-leaved lady fern, is a species of fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its delicate, straw-colored fronds.
